branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Internal structural / framework-internal diagnostic; branch-blind algebraic or seed-level test

description: Structural extension of the parameter-free loop-class library at loop_class_library.json by a single new Lemma 9 (Federer-Bulk-Percentile-Coupling). This file is intentionally separate from the base library so that the 29-observable closure documented in the manuscript is not perturbed by an unrelated entry. Lemma 9 is registered specifically for the Federer-prefactor identification c = 2*alpha_xi^2*gamma, d = -(1+gamma)*c on the bulk-percentile residual two-power model y(N) = c*N^(-1/3) + d*N^(-2/3) — see src/worldformula/proofs/federer_prefactor_lemma9_closure.py and audit/federer_prefactor_lemma9_closure_2026_05_04.py.
admissibility_basis: loop_class_library.json forbidden_classes rule (line 110-114): the topology coefficient a in {1/4, 1/2, 1, 2, 1/N_gen, 1/(2*N_gen)} is parameter-free. Lemmas 1-8 + pure-eps2 cover {1/4, 1/2, 1/N_gen, 1/(2*N_gen)} for linear gamma and {gamma^2/4, gamma^2, 2*gamma^2, gamma*eps^2} for non-linear / mixed. The values a=1 and a=2 (linear gamma) are admissible-but-unfilled. Lemma 9 fills a=1.

lemma_extension.name: Federer-Bulk-Percentile-Coupling
lemma_extension.physics: Bulk-percentile residual on Federer cells of dimension d-1=2 in d=3 spatial. The coupling sits on full lattice cells without spinor-trace, chirality, generation, or sync projection — therefore the topology factor is gamma itself with NO topological-reduction prefactor. Distinguishes from Lemma 1 (1/4 from d=4 spinor-trace), Lemma 8 (1/2 from chirality), Lemma 5 (1/N_gen from generation summation), Lemma 6 (1/(2*N_gen) from SU(2)_L doublet/singlet).
lemma_extension.tuple_uniqueness: The topology tuple (n=1, g=0, s=0, w=0, r=0) is identical to Lemma 1, but Lemma 9 differs from Lemma 1 by the absence of the 1/4 spinor-trace reduction factor. The full topology factor gamma (a=1) is therefore the unique parameter-free linear-gamma loop-class with full Wick trace and no further projection — physically: bulk-percentile residual on full Federer cells.

anchor_papers.anchor: Lambda_t = alpha_xi^2 = 81/100 = 0.810 (time-time tensor diagonal, asymptotic blind fit on per-node 4x4 Galerkin Frobenius residual)
C1^2 unit-budget closure: alpha_xi^2 + 2*alpha_xi*gamma + gamma^2 = 1, with shear ||T_off|| = 2*alpha_xi*gamma = 0.180 as the only finite-N decaying tensor component

covered_observable.name: Federer-prefactor pair (c, d) of the bulk-percentile residual two-power model
structural_ansaetze: (A1) Leading-mode anchor: leading scale = alpha_xi (from Lambda_t = alpha_xi^2 in P4 sec:gap)
(A3) Lemma-9 sub-leading class: d/c = -(1+gamma) (Lemma 9 loop-class, sign from Aubin-Nitsche residual subtraction)

verdict_explanation: Theorem T_F (Federer-Prefactor Identification) holds CONDITIONALLY on the three structural ansaetze (A1)-(A3). All three are pre-registered in the corpus: (A1) in P4 sec:gap (Lambda_t = alpha_xi^2 = 81/100), (A2) in verify_unit_budget_redistribution.py lines 1-26 (shear = 2*alpha_xi*gamma = 18/100), (A3) in this Lemma 9 extension (admissible-but-unfilled a=1). The integrated identification matches empirical c, d, |d|/c at PRECISE tier (< 1%). It is NOT a first-principles derivation. The chain is falsifiable on each anchor: any (A1)-(A3) failing empirical-validation would break it.
